jobs.description:
Visual Merchandiser & Trainer - Northeast - Swatch Group

Visual Merchandiser & Trainer - Northeast
The Company
Hamilton has been an iconic name in precision watchmaking since its founding in Lancaster, Pennsylvania in 1892. Its watches have synchronized the first railroads and furnished the wrists of soldiers, aviators and Hollywood stars, making over 500 appearances on the big screen. Hamilton has been at the forefront of innovations in design, technology and craft by launching the world’s first electric timepiece in 1957 and LED digital watch in 1970. A member of the Swatch Group, the global leader in watch manufacturing and distribution, Hamilton combines its American spirit with true Swiss precision.
Job Description
As a Visual Merchandiser & Trainer with Hamilton, U.S., the position will be responsible to support the watch sales within our retail and wholesale account through visual merchandising and training. This role will oversee the visual representation of our Hamilton brand and products in all boutiques (retail and wholesale) within assigned Northeast Territory region. Visual Merchandiser & Trainer will also be responsible for maintaining training initiatives that will increase Tissot brand and product knowledge. Tasks include the planning, organization, coordination, training and implementation of all Visual Merchandising measures that have been developed by the Tissot.
This position is a field position.
Profile
Visual Merchandising Responsibilities
• Implement and maintain visual merchandising standards across retail and wholesale store locations, ensuring compliance with Company guidelines and brand standards.
• Conduct regular store visits to evaluate visual execution, providing on-the-spot adjustments and recommendations.
• Contribute to achieving sales volume goals by ensuring effective presentations and maintenance of all product
categories, making sure that all stores are set up correctly during promotions and regular business.
• Prepare and distribute communication materials related to visual displays in accordance with brand guidelines.
• Manage vendor relationships by placing and following up on visual orders to ensure timely implementation and centralized mailing of visual materials to trade location.
• Coordinate internal distribution of visual materials to stores, ensuring compliance and visibility.
• Collaborate with retail locations, regional teams, and brand leadership to plan and execute floor sets, window displays, seasonal transitions, and promotional setups.
• Identify visual merchandising opportunities within and outside stores, developing innovative window decoration ideas.
• Create and maintain a photo library documenting boutique conditions before and after visits.
• Provide recap reports summarizing store visit findings and improvement recommendations.
• Assist Visual Manager and SIS Project Manager with identifying SIS opportunities and maintenance/renovation needs.
• Lead first-class setup and decoration of brand-sponsored events, mono- and multi-brand related, to maximize visual impact.
• Partner with management in planning and executing functional training, leadership development, product and sales training and other agreed training strategies.
Training & Development Responsibilities
• Facilitate training sessions for store managers and associates focused on current visual merchandising standards, product placement, and customer experience best practices.
• Provide comprehensive communication and training programs to stores
• Partner with management on planning and executing functional, leadership, product, and sales training programs.
• Provide ongoing coaching to store teams, identifying skill gaps and recommending development initiatives.
• Foster strong relationships with store teams to support adoption of visual and training standards.
Additional Responsibilities
• Monitor regional business trends, competitor trends by reading and understanding business reports and identify potential opportunities to support sales growth.
• Perform other duties as assigned by management.
Requirements
Education and Experience Requirements
• High School Diploma or GED required; College degree preferred.
• 2-3 years of retail visual merchandising experience, preferably in specialty goods, jewelry, or watches.
• Strong verbal and written communication skills.
• Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
• Valid driver’s license required.
• Proven ability to coach and train diverse teams in a fast-paced retail environment.
• Strong eye for design, layout, and brand storytelling through product presentation.
• Ability to prioritize multiple responsibilities and thrive in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.
• The work schedule will vary from Monday through Friday and may include an occasional weekend.
• Ability to domestically and internationally up to 80-85% of the time (minimum 4 days per workweek).
Physical Requirements:
This is primarily active position which requires the Visual Merchandiser & Trainer to move about the various locations while continuously climbing ladders, bending, stooping, lifting, reaching, moving and carrying up to 40 lbs. There is frequent travel to multiple retail locations within assigned territory. The position requires the ability to operate motor vehicles, computers and tablets, communicate clearly, and engage physically with visual merchandising setups. Finger dexterity is necessary. Occasional evening or weekend work to support store openings, seasonal transitions, or special events.
Additional Skill Requirements:
• Strong organizational, project management, and problem-solving skills.
• Confident communicator and presenter.
• Self-motivated, proactive, and a quick learner.
• Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
• Ability to remain engaged and responsive when working remotely.
• Flexibility to work extended hours when required.
• Willingness to accept feedback and continuously improve.
This description outlines the basic responsibilities and requirements for the role. This is not a comprehensive listing of all job duties of the Visual Merchandiser & Trainer. Duties, responsibilities and activities may evolve or change at any time
with or without notice.
Compensation & Benefits
The annual base salary range for this position is $70,000–$77,000. Actual compensation will be determined based on factors such as experience and skills. This position is also eligible for an annual bonus.
The Swatch Group is an equal opportunity employer that promotes diversity and is committed to environmental sustainability.
Excellent health, dental and vision insurance (employee and dependents are eligible 1st of the month following 30 days; generous employer contribution)
Immediate participation in the 401(k) and 100% vested employer match after one year (maximum 4% employer match with 5% employee contribution)
Company paid life insurance and Long Term Disability
18 days of PTO per year
Special bucket of sick time per year extended paid time for medical, parental and military leave
Holiday pay
Voluntary FSA, STD, Accident/Hospital Indemnity insurance
Employee product discount
For more information please see https://transparency-in-coverage.uhc.com/

jobs.description:
OMEGA Luxury Timepieces - Sales Associate - Dallas - Swatch Group

OMEGA Luxury Timepieces - Sales Associate - Dallas
The company
OMEGA is a company of the Swatch Group, the world’s leading watch manufacturer. Since 1848, the brand has been synonymous with excellence, innovation, design and precision. Thanks to its pioneering spirit, OMEGA was the first watch worn on the moon, and it has enjoyed many other accomplishments and explorations in the ocean and in space. Its women’s watches have been amongst the most admired and ingenious in the industry and, most recently, OMEGA’s reputation has been enhanced by its achievements in anti-magnetic watchmaking and its Master Chronometer certified watches, the industry’s highest standard for precision and performance. descriptionByLocale.de:
OMEGA Luxury Timepieces - Keyholder - Jacksonville - Swatch Group

OMEGA Luxury Timepieces - Keyholder - Jacksonville
The company
OMEGA is a company of the Swatch Group, the world’s leading watch manufacturer. Since 1848, the brand has been synonymous with excellence, innovation, design and precision. Thanks to its pioneering spirit, OMEGA was the first watch worn on the moon, and it has enjoyed many other accomplishments and explorations in the ocean and in space. Its women’s watches have been amongst the most admired and ingenious in the industry and, most recently, OMEGA’s reputation has been enhanced by its achievements in anti-magnetic watchmaking and its Master Chronometer certified watches, the industry’s highest standard for precision and performance. OMEGA has been the Official Timekeeper of 30 Olympic Games since 1932 and is also a leading name in golf, swimming, athletics, bobsleigh and sailing. Partnerships with social causes include Orbis International and its Flying Eye Hospital, as well as the GoodPlanet Foundation. Today, OMEGA's family of brand ambassadors includes James Bond, George Clooney, Nicole Kidman, Eddie Redmayne, Daniel Craig, Cindy Crawford, Michael Phelps, Rory McIlroy, Sergio Garcia, Buzz Aldrin and many more.
Job description
Ideal candidate will have relevant luxury retail sales experience and Keyholder or supervisory background. This position must possess exceptional customer service skills and a demeanor that embodies a great sense of approachability. Candidate should also have the ability to meet or exceed sales objectives and possess a strong desire to learn about our prestigious timepieces and looking to become an essential member of our team. The applicant of choice should have a proven track record of longevity with prior luxury employers.
• The Luxury Keyholder assists in leading retail operations, helps manage selling and supervises staff. They must possess the ability to build revenue and operational excellence.
• Profile
• For details on the prerequisites for this exciting opportunity, please see skillsets required
• MUST possess 3 years of Luxury retail Sales/Keyholder experience, preferably in the timepiece or fine jewelry field
• impressive selling capabilities and notable customer service skills
• Elevated ability to communicate effectively both written and orally
• Robust adaptability to learn the technical aspects of our company collection of timepieces
• Ability and willingness to train others in various areas of the business
• Talented negotiation prowess
• Passion for the art of selling
• Must be able to work flexible schedule throughout the week
• Local applicants only.
• Professional requirements
• Minimum 3 years of Luxury retail Sales/Keyholder experience, preferably in the timepiece or fine jewelry field
• Demonstrates leadership qualities in as much as representing the Manager’s vision at all times and ensures that policies and procedures are upheld at all times.
• MS Office experience is highly desirable
• Benefits Program
• The Swatch Group is an equal opportunity employer that promotes diversity and is committed to environmental sustainability.
• Excellent health, dental and vision insurance (employee and dependents are eligible 1st of the month following 30 days; generous employer contribution)
• Immediate participation in the 401(k) and 100% vested employer match after one year (maximum 4% employer match with 5% employee contribution)
• Company paid life insurance and Long Term Disability
• 18 PTO days per year, 23 PTO after 5 years
• 7 days per year extended paid time for medical, parental and military leave
• Holiday pay
• Voluntary FSA, STD, Accident/Hospital Indemnity insurance
• Employee product discount
• Transitchek Program – Save money on commuting to work and/or parking with up to $270 pre-tax dollars per month
• Internal transfer and growth potential
• $500 Employee Referral Bonuses

Polisher
The company
Swatch Group is the world's number one manufacturer of finished watches. With its 16 watch brands, the Group is present in all price segments, and is also active in the manufacture and sale of jewelry, watch movements and components.
Swatch Group unites, among other companies, the following watch brands under its roof: Breguet, Harry Winston, Blancpain, Glashütte Original, Jaquet Droz, Omega, Longines, Rado, Union Glashütte, Tissot, Balmain, Certina, Mido, Hamilton, Swatch and Flik Flak. The Group has a strong distribution and multi-brand retail net with two sophisticated boutiques under the name of Tourbillon and Hour Passion. Swatch Group is also an important player in advanced nanomechanical, nanoelectronic and Bluetooth technologies essential to watchmaking and other industries.

jobs.description:
Sales Coordinator - Swatch Group

Sales Coordinator
The company
Swatch Group unites, among other companies, the following watch brands under its roof: Breguet, Harry Winston, Blancpain, Glashütte Original, Jaquet Droz, Omega, Longines, Rado, Union Glashütte, Tissot, Balmain, Certina, Mido, Hamilton, Swatch and Flik Flak. The Group has a strong distribution and multi-brand retail net with two sophisticated boutiques under the name of Tourbillon and Hour Passion. Swatch Group is also an important player in advanced nanomechanical, nanoelectronic and Bluetooth technologies essential to watchmaking and other industries
Job description
We’re seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Sales Coordinator to join our team. As a Sales Coordinator, you’ll provide support to Brand Manager to ensure that all parts of the sales process run smoothly.
Sales Coordinator supports Brand Manager by preparing reports to help achieve sales targets, managing administrative tasks, analysing sales data, generating reports and maintaining accurate client records.
Key 
• Responsibilities and Duties
BM/Sales Team Support: Tracking Sales data, monitoring order status, handling inquiries, and ensuring timely delivery.
Reporting & Data Management: Sales data analysis, weekly/monthly reports, and maintaining accurate client records.
Interdepartmental Coordination: Liaising with marketing, finance, and logistics to streamline sales operations.
Profile
Experience: 2+ years in sales support / administration
Strong organization skills and excellent customer service skills.
Multitasking abilities with high attention to detail.
Professional requirements
Bachelor degree.
Technical Skills: Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
Languages
Excellent verbal and written communication skills for client interaction in Spanish and English
Other languages as a plus
